phase_1.audio: Audio recording captures initial spike of 73.8 dBA, gradually settling to 58.8-61.8 dBA midway through session. Vocalization detected mid-session (confidence: 0.93), not significantly affecting ambient baseline. Moderate noise levels present; some distraction possible but manageable.
phase_1.vitals: Vitals monitoring recorded 90 bpm as session average. This deviates from ideal focus-state parameters.
phase_1.gps_motion: Geolocation data exhibits high initial uncertainty (4.5m) that improves to 1.3m as session progresses. Accelerometer readings show user quickly becoming immobile and remaining seated throughout.

phase_2.correlations: GPS stabilization timing aligns closely with motion state change. Audio context remains stable throughout captured period.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: Session demonstrates strong focus-supportive conditions with low ambient noise (61.8 dB), minimal body repositioning.

phase_3.recommendations: Current 128s sessions reasonable; consider testing longer 30-min blocks.

reason: Concentration score reflects excellent audio conditions (61.8dB), minimal motion, and brief session length.
